message:
feat(search): move hash table (hcreate/hsearch/hdestroy) to core (bd-srch-4)

Sub-bead 4 of bd-srch-epic. The hash-table algorithm previously lived
in frankenlibc-abi/src/search_abi.rs (~80 lines of linear-probing,
djb2 hashing, slot scanning); this commit moves it into a new
generic frankenlibc-core/src/search/hash.rs module so it can be
unit-tested independently and reused.

Core API (no unsafe):
- LinearProbeTable<K, V>: open-addressed linear-probing table with
  pre-allocated slots and never-resize policy (matches glibc's
  hcreate which fixes capacity at create time)
- LinearSlot<K, V>: #[repr(C)] slot struct (key, value, occupied) so
  the abi layer can cast slot pointers to POSIX Entry*
- search(key, hash, eq) -> Option<usize>
- enter(key, value, hash, eq) -> Option<(idx, was_new)>
- slot_at / slot_mut_at / slot_address (raw pointer for FFI)
- djb2_seed() + djb2_step(seed, byte) helpers exposed for callers

Abi rewire:
- HashKey(#[repr(transparent)] *mut c_char) and HashData(*mut c_void)
  newtypes implementing Default + Clone + Copy (Send via unsafe impl
  bounded by the GLOBAL_HTAB Mutex)
- hash_key_djb2 walks the C string via core's djb2 helpers
- hash_keys_equal uses string_abi::strcmp
- hsearch / hsearch_r are now ~15 lines each, delegating to the core
  table and casting slot_address() to *mut Entry

5 core hash unit tests + 17 total core search tests pass; all 10
search conformance tests pass with no regression in behavior or
returned-pointer stability.

Closes bd-srch-4; bd-srch-5 will move lsearch/lfind to core.
